intro: Functional currency is the measurement lens associated with an entity's primary economic environment. AccountCcy treats it as a ledger-measurement discipline that must remain distinct from account currency, transaction currency, reporting currency, and consolidation currency.
doctrine_statement:
Functional currency explains the economic lens. Account currency governs account-level custody.
Confusing them weakens monetary reconstruction.
primary_section_title: Functional currency belongs to measurement, not every currency question.
primary_section_body: Functional currency is widely used in accounting standards and professional practice. AccountCcy references it carefully as established terminology, while the CCY State Chain remains the site's original reference architecture.

The key discipline is separation: transaction currency measures obligations, account currency assigns custody, ledger currency accumulates books, reporting currency presents results, and functional currency informs measurement policy.
This page is educational reference material only and does not determine functional currency for any entity.

pillar_sections.title: Functional currency relative to neighboring states

steps.title: Account currency
steps.body: Account-level custody setting for maintained balances.

steps.title: Ledger currency
steps.body: The books where functional measurement usually becomes operationally visible.

steps.title: Reporting currency
steps.body: Presentation currency for reporting packages, management views, or filings.

steps.title: Revaluation and translation
steps.body: Separate mechanics that often become confused when functional currency is poorly governed.

pillar_sections.title: Evidence that functional currency has been governed
paragraphs: A defensible posture includes a documented determination, review cadence, entity-level ownership, ERP configuration alignment, and close-period evidence showing that remeasurement and translation followed the approved lens.
The highest-risk failure is not disagreement about a term. It is a gap between policy memos, live ledger configuration, and close outputs.

pillar_sections.title: Functional currency control questions
questions: Does documented functional currency match ledger configuration and entity operating facts?
Who approves reassessment after acquisitions, market changes, or business model shifts?
How do remeasurement populations tie to functional currency policy?
Which reporting packages depend on translation from functional measurement?
Can reviewers connect policy, configuration, and close output without informal explanations?

graph_title: Functional currency is a measurement lens inside the custody chain.
graph_intro: The page stabilizes high-value definition intent while linking into AccountCcy's original state architecture.

cards.label: Policy
cards.title: Economic environment matters.
cards.body: Functional currency is determined through professional judgment under applicable standards.

cards.label: Configuration
cards.title: Policy must meet ERP reality.
cards.body: Memos alone do not prove measurement behavior if ledgers operate differently.

cards.label: Boundary
cards.title: Do not confuse measurement with custody.
cards.body: Account currency and functional currency can align, but they answer different control questions.

control_title: Tie functional currency policy to live ledger evidence.
control_body: Maintain determination memos, reassessment triggers, configuration snapshots, and close evidence together.
risk_title: Policy language detached from ledger behavior.
risk_body: Finance may cite functional currency correctly while the ERP operates under contradictory settings.

faq.question: Is functional currency the same as account currency?
faq.answer: No. Functional currency is an entity measurement lens; account currency is account-level custody inside the chart or ERP structure.

faq.question: Is this accounting advice?
faq.answer: No. It is educational reference content. Determination requires qualified professional judgment.
